mechanism_narrative: Persistently elevated federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.05 over a 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS is in the top quartile of its historical distribution.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals labor market slack, reduced household income, and weaker consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in triggered periods are not significantly negative (mean return > -0.5% with p > 0.10), or the signal's Rank IC falls below -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales decline month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains elevated, consumer spending contraction is compounded by tight monetary conditions, amplifying downside risk to equities over a 5-day horizon.
regime_hint: late-cycle demand weakness with tight money
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return in triggered instances is not negative, or more than 40% of triggered days produce positive returns over any out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-2008 recession recovery phase, rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing activity, driving corporate earnings and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails if, during any NBER-designated recovery period between 2009 and 2015, the Rank IC of INDPRO for 20-day forward returns is non-positive or below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP signals broad economic expansion, increasing corporate profits and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the correlation between GDPC1 quarterly change and 60-day forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) or the Rank IC over any 3-year window is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds a threshold of 3% year-over-year, it triggers expectations of monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if, in triggered periods, 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean < -0.5%, p < 0.10) or if the false positive rate exceeds 60% over any 2-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in payroll employment and industrial production confirm synchronized economic expansion across labor and manufacturing, boosting confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Hypothesis fails if the average 20-day forward return in jointly triggered months is not positive and significantly different from zero (p < 0.05), or if the signal's hit rate is below 55% over any 3-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: During the housing market recovery phase following the 2008 crash, rising housing starts signal improving construction activity, wealth effects, and residential investment, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Falsified if during the 2009-2013 housing recovery, the Rank IC of HOUST for 60-day forward returns is non-positive or below 0.04, or if the long-short spread from top vs. bottom HOUST deciles is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).
